problemTitle: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree

sources.code:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(h)

class Solution(object):
    # @param {TreeNode} root
    # @param {TreeNode} p
    # @param {TreeNode} q
    # @return {TreeNode}
    def lowestCommonAncestor(self, root, p, q):
        if root in (None, p, q):
            return root

        left, right = [self.lowestCommonAncestor(child, p, q) \
                         for child in (root.left, root.right)]
        # 1. If the current subtree contains both p and q,
        #    return their LCA.
        # 2. If only one of them is in that subtree,
        #    return that one of them.
        # 3. If neither of them is in that subtree,
        #    return the node of that subtree.
        return root if left and right else left or right
sources.originalCode:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(h)

class Solution(object):
    # @param {TreeNode} root
    # @param {TreeNode} p
    # @param {TreeNode} q
    # @return {TreeNode}
    def lowestCommonAncestor(self, root, p, q):
        if root in (None, p, q):
            return root

        left, right = [self.lowestCommonAncestor(child, p, q) \
                         for child in (root.left, root.right)]
        # 1. If the current subtree contains both p and q,
        #    return their LCA.
        # 2. If only one of them is in that subtree,
        #    return that one of them.
        # 3. If neither of them is in that subtree,
        #    return the node of that subtree.
        return root if left and right else left or right

# [Reader note] Entry point

class Solution(object):
    # @param {TreeNode} root
    # @param {TreeNode} p
    # @param {TreeNode} q
    # @return {TreeNode}
    def lowestCommonAncestor(self, root, p, q):
        if root in (None, p, q):
            return root

        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Postorder reasoning, return-value design, and distinguishing between 'found here' and 'found below.'
        left, right = [self.lowestCommonAncestor(child, p, q) \
                         # [Reader note] Main pass
                         # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
                         for child in (root.left, root.right)]
        # 1. If the current subtree contains both p and q,
        #    return their LCA.
        # 2. If only one of them is in that subtree,
        #    return that one of them.
        # 3. If neither of them is in that subtree,
        #    return the node of that subtree.
        # [Reader note] Decision point
        # Pay attention here: The statement is friendly, but weak return-value design can make the recursion messy fast.
        return root if left and right else left or right

sources.reviewedCode:
# Definition for a binary tree node.
# class TreeNode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Children send signals upward; the parent decides whether those signals combine into the answer.
#     def __init__(self, x):
# [Reader note] State setup
# This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Postorder reasoning, return-value design, and distinguishing between 'found here' and 'found below.'
#         self.val = x
#         self.left = None
#         self.right = None


class Solution:
    def lowestCommonAncestor(
        self, root: "TreeNode", p: "TreeNode", q: "TreeNode"
    ) -> "TreeNode":
        # [Reader note] Decision point
        # Pay attention here: The statement is friendly, but weak return-value design can make the recursion messy fast.
        if root in (None, p, q):
            return root
        left = self.lowestCommonAncestor(root.left, p, q)
        right = self.lowestCommonAncestor(root.right, p, q)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return root if left and right else (left or right)

writeups.title: 235.Lowest-Common-Ancestor-of-a-Binary-Search-Tree
writeups.excerpt: ### 235.Lowest-Common-Ancestor-of-a-Binary-Search-Tree 从根节点开始考察，如果p,q都比root小，则root移动至其左子树；否则，root移动至其右子树。直到发现p,q在root节点的两侧，则root是最低共同节点。 ### 236.Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree #### 解法1：找到各自完全的路径 和235不同，没有任何线索提示这两个节点的位置关系。所以只好用DFS搜索出抵达这两个节点的路径。通过路径的比较，找出共同的一段path，即可得出最低的公共节点。 DFS函数会一路向下层搜索直到发现节点p，然后在返回的过程中将路径所经过的所有节点存在数组P中。 ```cpp bool DFS(TreeNode* node, TreeNode* p, vector<TreeNode*>&P) { if (node==NULL) r...
